subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the main idea and message of the poem 'Amanda'
Identify the conflict between Amanda and her parent
Recognize the theme of freedom and control
Understand the use of imagination and fantasy in the poem
Develop basic comprehension, speaking, and creative writing skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poem and Poet
teaching_strategy: Briefly introduce the poet and her writing style
Ask students about rules they follow at home
Discuss how children feel when constantly instructed
student_activities: Predict what the poem might be about from its title
Share personal experiences about being corrected or guided by parents
assessment: Oral Q&A about the poet and topic

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Basic Understanding
topics_covered: Understanding difficult words: languid, tranquil, sulking
Identifying the speaker in different stanzas
teaching_strategy: Read the poem with proper expression
Explain parent’s lines and Amanda’s imaginary lines separately
Write meanings of difficult words on the board
student_activities: Identify the imaginary parts written in brackets
assessment: Ask students who is speaking in each stanza

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Theme and Imagination
topics_covered: Amanda’s imaginary roles: mermaid, orphan, Rapunzel
teaching_strategy: Discuss why Amanda escapes into fantasy
Create a simple comparison chart (Reality vs Imagination)
student_activities: List the three imaginary characters Amanda imagines
Write one reason why she chooses each role

lesson_plan.title: Literary Devices and Tone
topics_covered: Simple introduction to imagery and contrast
teaching_strategy: Explain how repetition creates a nagging tone
Give examples of imagery from the poem
assessment: Oral Q&A on tone and repetition

lesson_plan.title: Creative Expression and Reflection
teaching_strategy: Summarize key points of the poem
Encourage students to relate poem to their life
student_activities: Write a short diary entry as Amanda
assessment: Review drawings for connection with theme

teaching_aids: Printed or projected text of the poem
Simple illustrations of mermaid, orphan, and Rapunzel
teacher_tip: Keep the lesson interactive and sensitive. Encourage students to express their feelings about rules and freedom respectfully. Focus on simple explanations and ensure students understand the difference between imagination and reality in the poem.
